T ECHNICAL S KILLS
Languages: Python, C++, JavaScript, HTML, CSS

Computer Vision & OCR: OpenCV, PaddleOCR, ELA, Forgery Localization, Object Detection
Backend: FastAPI, Flask, REST APIs, Pydantic
Frontend: React.js, HTML/CSS
Databases & Tools: MongoDB, Git, GitHub, Linux, VS Code, Jupyter, Google Colab, Roboflow, Hugging Face
E XPERIENCE
NOVAC
Jun 2026 - Jul 2026
AI/Software Development Intern
Chennai, India
Fraudulent Document Detection: Built a full-stack AI fraud-detection platform for PDFs/images using React, FastAPI, MongoDB, and
Python, supporting uploads, automated analysis, history tracking, result storage, and PDF report generation.
Integrated PaddleOCR, ELA, MVSS-Net, TruFor, document-quality scoring, and custom rule-based logic into a unified document fraud-risk
pipeline.
Improved system reliability and inference speed using model caching, shared preprocessing, PDF-to-image conversion tuning, and
separated model environments.
Vehicle Damage Detection: Trained EfficientNetB0 and MobileNetV2 models via transfer learning on Roboflow and Kaggle vehicle-
damage datasets to classify damage severity for insurance-claim assessment.
AI Hiring Platform (Resume-cum-Internship Bot): Built an AI-powered hiring platform with a FastAPI backend and React dashboard to
automate resume parsing, identity-document verification, and skill-based candidate scoring, integrating Hugging Face models and OCR to
extract and validate candidate data and rank applicants by skills, experience, and job relevance.
Google Developer Student Clubs (GDSC), NIT Calicut
Dec 2025 - Present
AI Member
Calicut, India
Working on a classroom energy optimization concept using occupancy and environmental patterns to identify inefficient energy use and
support data-driven, adaptive allocation of lighting and cooling resources.
Participate in AI/ML-focused technical discussions, workshops, and student-led project initiatives.
P ROJECTS
Quantitative Trading Backtester | C++, Python, pandas, matplotlib GitHub
Built a modular C++ backtesting engine for rule-based trading strategies, including moving-average crossover and SMA-based entry/exit
systems.
Implemented transaction costs, cooldown periods, stop-loss logic, trailing exits, and position sizing for realistic trade simulation.
Calculated total return, Sharpe ratio, maximum drawdown, and trade-level profit/loss; used Python to visualize equity curves and compare
strategy performance against benchmarks.
Fake News Classification using Neural Networks | Python, NumPy, NLP GitHub
Built a fake news classification pipeline from scratch using text preprocessing, tokenization, and TF-IDF-based feature extraction on a
labeled news dataset.
Hand-coded logistic regression and a fully connected neural network in raw NumPy - with no machine-learning libraries - implementing
manual forward propagation and backpropagation.
Tuned vocabulary size, feature scaling, and regularization; evaluated performance using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and
confusion matrices, with bigram/trigram features improving results.
A CHIEVEMENTS & L EADERSHIP
Class Representative - Mechanical Engineering, NIT Calicut (Dec 2024 - Apr 2025).
Finalist, Big Fight 2026 Debate Competition - Debating Society, NIT Calicut.
Best Delegate, Srimutha MUN (2022) - Recognized for outstanding performance at a school-level Model United Nations conference.
Member, Montage - Filmmaking club of NIT Calicut; contributed to creative storytelling, script development, visual planning, and short-
form reels.
Member, Google Developer Student Club, NIT Calicut - AI/ML-focused technical engagement and projects.

q2.answer_text:
At NOVAC, I worked on a document fraud detection system focused on verifying uploaded identity and financial documents. My role was mainly in building and integrating the backend AI pipeline, connecting OCR, fraud detection logic, document quality checks, and the final result/report generation into a usable FastAPI-based application.

The pipeline started with a user uploading a document through the frontend. The FastAPI backend received the file, handled image/PDF preprocessing, and passed it through PaddleOCR to extract text fields such as name, ID number, date of birth, and other document-specific information. After OCR, I added field-level validation logic to check whether extracted values were consistent, whether important fields were missing or masked, and whether the document looked suspicious.

Along with OCR, the system also used computer vision and forgery detection components such as ELA, MVSS, and TruFor-style localization to detect tampered regions, pasted text, altered fields, or poor-quality/synthetic documents. The challenge was that OCR and forgery models behave differently on images and PDFs, so I had to handle PDF-to-image conversion, bounding box alignment, preprocessing, model timeouts, and result normalization carefully.

I also worked on making the backend more stable and faster. Heavy models were isolated using separate virtual environments and called through subprocesses where needed, so the main FastAPI server would not break due to dependency conflicts. I added caching, route-level error handling, structured JSON responses, and timing logs to understand bottlenecks.

On the frontend side, I helped display the analysis results clearly, including risk score, detected mismatches, document quality indicators, and downloadable PDF reports. Overall, the project taught me how to combine OCR, computer vision models, backend APIs, and frontend reporting into a deployable end-to-end fraud detection system rather than just a standalone ML model.

strengths: Technical correctness in explaining the technology stack and role
Clarity in describing the project workflow
Depth of explanation covering multiple aspects of the project

questions.question: Can you tell me about your role at NOVAC as an AI/Software Development Intern?
Can you explain how you used TensorFlow/Keras in one of your projects?
How would you integrate PaddleOCR into a document fraud detection system using FastAPI and React?
Describe the architecture of your AI Hiring Platform, including how you integrated Hugging Face models and OCR.
Given that your resume mentions missing skills in Java and SQL, how do you plan to address these gaps during your time at the company?

q1.answer_text:
At NOVAC, my role as an AI/Software Development Intern was to build and integrate applied AI features into a document verification and fraud detection system. I worked on the backend pipeline, model integration, OCR-based field extraction, frontend result display, and overall debugging of the system.

The main project involved detecting fraudulent or tampered documents. The user could upload an image or PDF, and the backend processed it through multiple stages. First, I used OCR tools like PaddleOCR to extract important document fields such as name, ID number, date of birth, and other visible text. Then I added validation logic to check whether the extracted fields were missing, inconsistent, masked, or suspicious.

Along with OCR, I also worked with computer vision and forgery detection components such as ELA, MVSS, and TruFor-style localization to identify manipulated regions in documents. One major challenge was handling both images and PDFs properly, because PDFs had to be converted into images before analysis, and bounding boxes had to remain accurate after conversion.

On the software side, I used FastAPI to structure the backend routes and services, handled file uploads, created structured JSON responses, and connected the analysis output to the React frontend. I also worked on improving the result page and PDF report generation so that the fraud score, detected issues, field mismatches, and document quality indicators were presented clearly.

A major part of my role was debugging and optimization. Some models were heavy and had dependency conflicts, so I separated model environments and used subprocess-based execution where needed. I also added caching, timing logs, error handling, and fallback logic to make the system more stable.

Overall, my role was not just training or testing one model, but building an end-to-end AI pipeline where OCR, computer vision, backend APIs, frontend UI, and report generation worked together as one deployable product.

strengths: Addresses the role and projects at NOVAC comprehensively
Provides specific details on backend, frontend, and model integration
weaknesses: Could have provided more technical depth on models used
Less emphasis on training or testing models directly

questions.question: Can you tell me about your role at NOVAC during your internship?
Describe the process of building the full-stack AI fraud-detection platform you worked on.
Can you explain how you integrated PaddleOCR into your document fraud detection system?
How would you design a deployable pipeline for an AI hiring platform, considering the integration of Hugging Face models and OCR?
Can you walk us through the process of training your EfficientNetB0 model for vehicle damage detection using transfer learning?
questions.expected_focus: Technical correctness, model or framework knowledge, practical depth

q1.answer_text:
At NOVAC, my role as an AI/Software Development Intern was to build and integrate applied AI features into a document verification and fraud detection system. I worked on the backend pipeline, model integration, OCR-based field extraction, frontend result display, and overall debugging of the system.

The main project involved detecting fraudulent or tampered documents. The user could upload an image or PDF, and the backend processed it through multiple stages. First, I used OCR tools like PaddleOCR to extract important document fields such as name, ID number, date of birth, and other visible text. Then I added validation logic to check whether the extracted fields were missing, inconsistent, masked, or suspicious.

Along with OCR, I also worked with computer vision and forgery detection components such as ELA, MVSS, and TruFor-style localization to identify manipulated regions in documents. One major challenge was handling both images and PDFs properly, because PDFs had to be converted into images before analysis, and bounding boxes had to remain accurate after conversion.

On the software side, I used FastAPI to structure the backend routes and services, handled file uploads, created structured JSON responses, and connected the analysis output to the React frontend. I also worked on improving the result page and PDF report generation so that the fraud score, detected issues, field mismatches, and document quality indicators were presented clearly.

A major part of my role was debugging and optimization. Some models were heavy and had dependency conflicts, so I separated model environments and used subprocess-based execution where needed. I also added caching, timing logs, error handling, and fallback logic to make the system more stable.

Overall, my role was not just training or testing one model, but building an end-to-end AI pipeline where OCR, computer vision, backend APIs, frontend UI, and report generation worked together as one deployable product.

strengths: Comprehensive answer covering multiple aspects of the role
Detailed explanation of technical tools and processes
weaknesses: Could have provided more specific examples or code snippets for clarity

questions.question: Can you tell me about your role at NOVAC during the summer of 2026?
What was the main challenge you faced while building your fake news classification pipeline, and how did you overcome it?
Describe how you implemented model caching in your document fraud detection system at NOVAC.
Explain the architecture of your AI hiring platform. How did you ensure data privacy and security in handling sensitive documents?
Tell me about a difficult bug or model integration issue you faced and how you systematically debugged it.
questions.expected_focus: Debugging discipline, ownership, communication under uncertainty

q1.answer_text:
At NOVAC, my role as an AI/Software Development Intern was to build and integrate applied AI features into a document verification and fraud detection system. I worked on the backend pipeline, model integration, OCR-based field extraction, frontend result display, and overall debugging of the system.

The main project involved detecting fraudulent or tampered documents. The user could upload an image or PDF, and the backend processed it through multiple stages. First, I used OCR tools like PaddleOCR to extract important document fields such as name, ID number, date of birth, and other visible text. Then I added validation logic to check whether the extracted fields were missing, inconsistent, masked, or suspicious.

Along with OCR, I also worked with computer vision and forgery detection components such as ELA, MVSS, and TruFor-style localization to identify manipulated regions in documents. One major challenge was handling both images and PDFs properly, because PDFs had to be converted into images before analysis, and bounding boxes had to remain accurate after conversion.

On the software side, I used FastAPI to structure the backend routes and services, handled file uploads, created structured JSON responses, and connected the analysis output to the React frontend. I also worked on improving the result page and PDF report generation so that the fraud score, detected issues, field mismatches, and document quality indicators were presented clearly.

A major part of my role was debugging and optimization. Some models were heavy and had dependency conflicts, so I separated model environments and used subprocess-based execution where needed. I also added caching, timing logs, error handling, and fallback logic to make the system more stable.

Overall, my role was not just training or testing one model, but building an end-to-end AI pipeline where OCR, computer vision, backend APIs, frontend UI, and report generation worked together as one deployable product.

strengths: Addresses the role and project at NOVAC
Provides detailed technical information about backend, frontend, and model integration
weaknesses: Could have provided more specific details on models used
Brief on some of the optimization techniques mentioned
